sections.translation:
## Studio–Transmitter Link

- In studios, the console with **/AoIP** option operates as an **encoder** and generates an audio stream to the transmitter site.
- At the transmitter site, a **decoder** receives the stream and sends it to the **audio processor** (for example Solidyne **ADA102**).

  Some FM processors can receive and decode the audio stream (for example Solidyne 542APC /AoIP)

**Network requirements:**

- Studio and Transmitter on the **same LAN** with a point-to-point link (physical or wireless), or
- **VPN** over high-performance Internet (consult with network technician).

---
sections.title: Enlace Estudio–Planta

sections.translation:
## Connection and Access

**Procedure:**

1. Connect the console to the network using an **Ethernet** cable.
2. Check the **LEDs** on the Ethernet connector: they indicate that the AoIP modules are **powered by USB** (at least one USB to PC powered on or an external USB source). The LEDs should blink.
3. The network assigns an **IP via DHCP**. With the obtained IP, the green RJ45 LED will blink.\
   If there is no DHCP, the module will search for a **free IP** (this may take a few minutes).
4. Obtain the IP using **Solidyne Multi-Discovery**.

  <Note>
     **Solidyne Multi-Discovery**

     If physical access to the console is not available, you can [**download the Multi-Discovery tool**](/en-US/DX816/816-discovery) which detects Solidyne devices connected to the network.
</Note>

5. Open a browser (Firefox/Chrome) and enter the **IP** found. The **IP Control Panel** will appear.

---
sections.title: Conexión y acceso

sections.translation: ### **Destination IP (Basic settings)** The initial status screen shows the IP port configuration. The factory default setting is _Encoder: Studio Encoder_ (option "Location" → 'Studio Encoder'). To verify the configuration, access the **"Configuration"** option and select **"Basic settings"** from the left menu. | Parameter | Configuration / Description | | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | | Location (Site type) | Factory default set to **Studio Encoder** (studio encoder). | | Stream method | **Push (RTP)** | | URL | Enter **destination IP or DNS** and **streaming port**. | | Apply | Press to confirm values. |
sections.title: **IP destino (Basic settings)**

sections.translation: ### Audio Options Access: **Configuration → Audio**. | Parameter | Factory Default / Recommended | Notes | | ------------- | ----------------------------- | ---------------------------------- | | Input source | Line Stereo | Do not modify. | | Format | PCM 16-bit stereo | Default. | | Sample rate | 44.1 kHz | Default. 48 kHz not supported. | | Bitrate (PCM) | ~1.6 Mbit/s | For PCM16@48 kHz stereo. |
sections.title: Opciones de audio

sections.translation:
### Status Screen

| Field                   | Description                                                                        |
| ----------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Site type               | Operation mode (e.g., **Studio Encoder** for Studio).                              |
| Stream mode             | Event/method configured to initiate transmission to remote.                        |
| Keep-alive              | Active connection maintenance strategy.                                            |
| Connection status       | Link status. **Green** = OK with destination IP; **Red** = connection issue.       |
| Incoming Stream         | Status of incoming stream (inactive in unidirectional encoder).                    |
| Outgoing Stream         | Status of outgoing stream (active in encoder).                                     |
| Audio input             | Audio source used (analog).                                                       |
| Audio format            | Format applied to transmitted audio (e.g., PCM16/48k).                            |
| Input/Output audio level | Actual audio levels (dBFS) for input/output.                                       |
| Relay 1 .. 4            | Not implemented.                                                                  |
| Remote inputs           | Not implemented.                                                                  |
| Local inputs            | Status of local inputs.                                                           |
| Local relays            | Not implemented.                                                                  |
sections.title: Pantalla de estado

sections.translation:
## Microwave Digital Link

For distances of **up to 45 km**, a point-to-point data link in **5.8 GHz** (or **2.4 GHz** depending on the country), **802.11.x** standard, can be used:

- **License-free** band, no special authorizations required.
- Allows transport of **PCM 16-bit / 44.1 kHz** audio (uncompressed) or **encoded** audio.
- Supports **bidirectional connection** for special applications.

<Info>
  For more details: [**info@solidynepro.com**](mailto:info@solidynepro.com)
</Info>

---
sections.title: Enlace digital de microondas

sections.translation:
## AoIP Module Power Supply

- AoIP modules are powered via **USB**; they require at least **one USB** connection to a **powered-on PC**.
- Without a USB-connected PC, or with the PC turned off, the IP service **will not be available**.

<Note>
  If no PC is connected, you may use a **standard USB power supply (5 V)** to power the AoIP modules.
</Note>
sections.title: Alimentación de módulos AoIP
